---
title: "Jinu's Cloud Seeding Blog"
lang: en
page-layout: full
title-block-style: none
---

::: {.home-intro}
<div class="home-kicker">Cloud seeding · Numerical modeling · Open notebook</div>
<h1>Computing cloud seeding,<br><em>documenting research.</em></h1>
<p class="home-deck">I explore cloud-seeding problems with PySDM and WRF, then revisit the literature in the language of experiments. The questions, assumptions, failures, revisions, and results are documented along the way.</p>
<div class="home-actions">
  <a href="pysdm.html">Browse PySDM notes <span aria-hidden="true">↗</span></a>
  <a href="about.html">About this blog <span aria-hidden="true">→</span></a>
</div>
:::

## Three research tracks {.section-heading}

:::: {.track-list}
::: {.track-item}
<div class="track-index">01</div>
### PySDM

Connect the Super-Droplet Method, particle-based cloud microphysics, code, and physical assumptions.

[Browse PySDM notes](pysdm.qmd)
:::

::: {.track-item}
<div class="track-index">02</div>
### WRF

Connect mesoscale weather fields with cloud processes and document reproducible WRF configurations and results.

[Browse WRF notes](wrf.qmd)
:::

::: {.track-item}
<div class="track-index">03</div>
### Article Review

Read the questions, methods, assumptions, and limitations of cloud-seeding literature from an experimental perspective.

[Browse article reviews](article-review.qmd)
:::
::::

## Recent notes {.section-heading}

::: {.manual-post-list}
::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-16 · Jinu</div>

### [Qualifying Rain-Producing Experiments and Letting the Server Work Overnight](posts/build-the-lab/2026-07-16-rain-server-execution/index.qmd)

Why a converged absolute rain state does not yet make the seeding-minus-control response trustworthy—and how the Lab now runs persistent, resource-bounded server experiments.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-15 · Jinu</div>

### [Using WRF to Ask Where and How Widely Hygroscopic Seeding Should Occur](posts/article-review/2026-07-15-wrf-hygroscopic-seeding/index.qmd)

Reviewing altitude, treated area, particle size, and the warm-rain pathway in a hybrid microphysics model.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-15 · Jinu</div>

### [Connecting Aerosol Hygroscopicity and CCN Activity with One κ](posts/article-review/2026-07-15-kappa-kohler/index.qmd)

The κ-Köhler equation as a bridge between aerosol water uptake, CCN activation, and PySDM experiments.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-15 · Jinu</div>

### [Starting with PySDM: First Coordinates for Cloud Microphysics Experiments](posts/learn-pysdm/2026-07-15-first-steps/index.qmd)

Introducing the Super-Droplet Method and PySDM, then mapping the learning and experimentation path ahead.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-14 · Jinu</div>

### [From Code That Produces Results to Code That Makes Them Trustworthy](posts/build-the-lab/2026-07-14-research-quality/index.qmd)

How diagnostic provenance, numerical qualification, failure evidence, and portable reports turned PySDM Seeding Lab into a research tool.
:::

::: {.manual-post}
<div class="manual-post-meta">2026-07-13 · Jinu</div>

### [Turning PySDM Experiment Scripts into a Seeding Lab](posts/build-the-lab/2026-07-13-scenario-workflow/index.qmd)

Designing a traceable Scenario → Sweep → Run → Dashboard workflow for cloud-seeding experiments.
:::
:::
